children.text: provide courses of instruction on navigation, marine engineering (including steam and diesel propulsion), the operation and maintenance of new vessels and equipment, and innovations being introduced to the merchant marine of the United States;

agree in writing to conform to the standards for courses, training facilities, admissions, and instruction that the Secretary of Transportation may establish after consultation with the superintendents of State maritime academies, which shall include standards for a program described in subsection (c);

agree in writing to require, as a condition for graduation, that each individual who is a citizen of the United States and who is attending the academy in a merchant marine officer preparation program pass the examination required for the issuance of a license under ; andsection 7101 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: required for the issuance of an original license under section 7101; or

children.label: ii
children.text: set by the Coast Guard for issuing merchant mariners’ documentation under section 7302, with no limit to the individual’s operational authority;

children.label: B
children.text: following passage of the examination under subparagraph (A), shall continue to meet the requirements described in subparagraph (A) throughout the remainder of the individual’s enrollment at the State maritime academy; and

children.label: C
children.text: if the individual has a medical or physical condition that disqualifies the individual from meeting the requirements referred to in subparagraph (A), shall be transferred to a program other than a merchant marine officer preparation program, or otherwise appropriately disenrolled from such State maritime academy, until the individual demonstrates to the Secretary that the individual meets such requirements.

children.heading: $25,000.—Additional Condition to Payments of More Than
children.label: b $25,000.—Additional Condition to Payments of More Than
children.text: As a condition of receiving an annual payment of more than $25,000 under , a State maritime academy also must agree to admit each year a number of citizens of the United States who meet its admission requirements and reside in a State not supporting that academy. The Secretary shall determine the number of individuals to be admitted by each academy under this subsection. The number may not be more than one-third of the total number of individuals attending the academy at any time.section 51505 of this title

children.heading: Armed Forces Merchant Mariner Officer Expedited Preparation Program.—
children.label: c Armed Forces Merchant Mariner Officer Expedited Preparation Program.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A State maritime academy may offer a program under which an eligible individual may complete a merchant marine officer preparation program approved by the Secretary, and the requirements for the issuance of a license under , in less than 3 years, without a requirement to earn a baccalaureate or other degree from the State maritime academy.section 7101 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a person who served in the Armed Forces, and who was discharged or released therefrom under honorable conditions; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a member of the National Guard or Reserves who has performed at least six years of service therein; and

children.label: B
children.text: who has earned a baccalaureate degree from an institution of higher education (as defined in section 102 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ()).20 U.S.C. 1002

children.heading: .—Secretarial Waiver Authority
children.label: d .—Secretarial Waiver Authority
children.text: The Secretary may modify or waive any of the terms set forth in subsection (a)(4) with respect to any individual or State maritime academy.
